Tests that adding Infinity and negative Infinity results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of adding `Infinity` and `-Infinity` in JavaScript. The `assert` function checks if the result of this operation is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected outcome according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on verifying a specific arithmetic operation involving special numeric values. It is likely part of a larger test suite that ensures the correct implementation of mathematical operations in a JavaScript environment.
